MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299927 The Roger W. Sleight 2010 Trust and
PTR-19-303227 The Roger W. sleight Trust.

Appearances; Mark Malachowski for Zachary Sleight, Sharon Sleight and Joshua Sleight (Petitioners/Objectors).
James Finsten appeared by zoom for and with Marilyn Bessey (Co-Trustee) by zoom.
Laura O'Kane and Fey Epling appeared by zoom for Mark Backer (Co-Trustee/Respondent).

The Court informed the Parties that this was put on his calendar in error, that there are several motions on Judge Quinn's calendar and that the Probate Examiner has raised the issue of appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL"). Mr. Malachowski stated that he was hoping to get some rulings today. The Court stated that it is not prepared to issue any rulings today and asked if anyone is objecting to Ms. O'Kane filing a petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem. Mr. Finsten stated he agrees there will be a mediation and some adjustment to the trust, that they were not aware of any Minors and that Ms. O'Kane has requested that they not contact any member of the Backer family. Ms. O'Kane stated that appointment of a GAL is appropriate, that the fees for the GAL will affect the trust estate and that she was told by her Client that the family felt they were being harassed by Ms. Bessey. Mr. Malachowski and Mr. Finsten indicated that they do not oppose/object to appointment of a GAL. Ms. O'Kane stated that Mr. Malachowski filed a motion to be relieved as Counsel indicating that there has been no communication and a complete breakdown in the Attorney Client relationship, that it is on calendar for February 9th, that there is a contentious of the peculiar relationship with Ms. Bessey and asked if the Court on its own motion could advance the hearing. Mr. Malachowski stated that the Sleights are in pro per in the civil case, that they filed a dismissal with prejudice, that he is asking to be removed in the probate case, that this is the date the Clerks came back with and that the Clients are seeking other Counsel. The Court stated that it will not advance the hearing, that there is a chance that a substitution of Counsel may be coming and that there is an ex parte procedure to advance hearing dates. Discussion was also held regarding the dismissal only having one Party's signature. Mr. Malachowski stated that he has signatures from all three of the Clients, that he will mail the documents to Counsel and that he needs to have a Judge tell him if he has to sit for depositions, or not. The Petition to Contest and Set Aside Purported Trust, etc., the Petition to Disqualify Laura O'Kane, etc., the First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc., the Report and First and Final Account of Mark Backer, etc., and the Petition for Instructions Regarding Invasion of Principal, etc., are continued to December 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Reporter: Mary Bardellini, CSR #2976, 530-320-2222, bardellini@sbcglobal.net

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Co-Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michele McKee (via Court Call) for (petitioner) Mark Backer.

The Court stated that the issue is bond, that there is a professional co-trustee and substantial real property. Ms. McKee reported that the other co-trustee is the sole heir and is living in one of the properties, that they would not be selling the property and that they would like to only have to bond for the liquid assets which would be a bond in the amount of $1,500,000.00. The Court stated that this would be acceptable if the trustees did not have the power to sell the real property. Ms. McKee stated that they are not willing to give up that power and that they are trying to save money. The Court pointed out that the bond is expensive due to the assets that it would cover. Ms. McKee stated that the property would not be sold without the consent of the co-trustee, that there could be a limitation that if something happens to one of the trustees one of the trustees the other could not sell the property. The Court granted the petition for appointment of successor co-trustee with full bond in the amount of $6,609,000.00 and signed the order in open court. Status on filing bond is set for October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
